author: jameshe5018
content: i just had it as a switch over without having to introduce another version 🤷‍♂️

author: nflaig
content: I am mostly missing the rationale for removing it, it seems simpler to keep existing spec which most clients have implemented since bellatrix. One use case I can see why you wanna configure this on a per validator basis is for staking as a service providers that use a single vc as a multi-tenant instance. I don't actually know if people do this, but in [#608](https://github.com/ethereum/execution-apis/pull/608) it was noted by Jim that we wanna configure gas limits per validator, so I would like <@&1337018366795186237> to chime in on this, also <@737740104281489531>. For lodestar, it would be easier to just keep as is, we don't have an issue setting per validator gas limits in gloas. Also <@219015859182632960> I know eth-docker can configure gas limit per validator with `ethd` cli. So in any case, removing this in gloas will introduce tooling churn.

author: bez625
content: Thanks for the tag <@586161934425128960> . You are correct - vouch allows setting of individual gas limits per validators and this is something we do utilise on mainnet. We'd rather preserve this and not have to change the spec in gloas.

author: serenita_io
content: I agree with Nico's primary objection – I don't see a good reason to remove this ability for validators. We've supported it for years, why would we now suddenly remove it with Gloas?

author: nflaig
content: it's the opposite, in gloas we will have https://github.com/ethereum/execution-apis/pull/796, so the CL is fully in control now and decides the target gas limit also for local payloads
